content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
• 428 .J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Rob Epstein 
City Attorney 
Office of the City Attorney 
1400 Fifth Avenue 
San Rafael, California 94901 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-16-126 
Dear Mr. Epstein: 
July 17, 2016 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of 
the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 
Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the 
Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of 
interest or Section 1090. 
QUESTION 
Does Council Member Gamblin have a conflict of interest in participating in either a council 
subcommittee that reviews potential homeless service center sites, including 67 Mark Drive, or in 
council meetings that involve a decision regarding a land use application for a proposed homeless 
services center at 67 Mark Drive? 
CONCLUSION 
Yes. Under the facts provided, participation in a subcommittee and making a land use 
decision concerning 67 Mark Drive will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on 
Council Member Gamblin's real property interest. 
FACTS 
You are the City Attorney for the City of San Rafael and you write on behalf of Council 
Member John Gamblin. 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Go\'ernmcnt Code Sections 8 I 000 through 91014. All stalUlory 
references arc to 1hc Gmernmcn1 Code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission arc contained in Sec1ions 18110 lhrough 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references arc to Title 2. Division 6 or the California Code of Regulations. unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. A-16-126 
Page No. 2 
City staff has been working with a local homeless services provider, along with the County 
of Marin, in reviewing various potential sites for a proposed homeless services center. One such site 
is 67 Mark drive in San Rafael. Council Member Gamblin's residence is located within 500 feet of 
the Mark Drive site. The driving distance between his residence and the Mark Drive site is 1.5 
miles, because the two properties are separated by a creek, and there is no direct access. The Mark 
Drive property cannot be viewed from the Gamblin residence. The building located at 67 Mark 
drive is currently office and warehouse space. As indicated in our follow up conversation, the 
proposed homeless services center will most likely include temporary shelter beds and would 
require a conditional use permit. The homeless services center might also include some permanent 
housing, which would require a zoning change. In addition, the center would include outdoor use, 
such as a play area for children. However, there has been no formal proposal at this point in time. 
ANALYSIS 
The Act's conflict-of-interest rules prohibit a public official from making, participating in 
making, or using his or her official position in any way to influence a governmental decision in 
which the official knows, or has reason to know, that he or she has a "financial interest." (Section 
87100.) A public official has a financial interest in a decision within the meaning of Section 87103 
if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable 
from its effect on the public generally, on the official, a member of his or her immediate family, or 
on any one of five enumerated economic interests. (Section 87103.) 
Financial lntere.\·t 
The interest here is real property in which Council Member Gamblin has an interest of 
$2,000 or more. (Section 87103(b).) 
Foreseeability and Materiality 
A financial effect is presumed to be reasonably foreseeable when it is explicitly involved in 
a decision. Financial interests that are explicitly involved include an interest that is a named party 
in, or subject of, a government decision. (Regulation 18701(a).) Based on the facts provided, 
Council Member Gamblin's financial interests are not explicitly involved in the decisions involving 
the proposed homeless services center. For interests that are not explicitly involved, as here, the 
effect need not be likely to be considered reasonably foreseeable. "In general, if the financial effect 
can be recognized as a realistic possibility and more than hypothetical or theoretical, it is reasonably 
foreseeable." (Regulation 1870l(b).) 
Real Property Interests: For real property not explicitly involved in a decision and pertinent 
to your facts, a foreseeable effect is material if the decision: 
"( I 0) Would change the character of the parcel of real property by substantially 
altering traffic levels or intensity of use, including parking, of property 
surrounding the official's real property parcel, the view, privacy, noise levels, or 
air quality, including odors, or any other factors that would affect the market 
value of the real property parcel in which the official has a financial interest; 


File No. A-16-126 
Page No. 3 
"( 1 1) Would consider any decision affecting real property value located within 
500 feet of the property line of the official's real property ... Notwithstanding this 
prohibition, the Commission may provide written advice allowing an official to 
participate under these circumstances if the Commission determines that there are 
sufficient facts to indicate that there will be no reasonably foreseeable measurable 
impact on the official's property; 
"( 12) Would cause a reasonably prudent person, using due care and consideration 
under the circumstances, to believe that the governmental decision was of such a 
nature that its reasonably foreseeable effect would influence the market value of 
the official's property." (Regulation 18702.2(a)( 10) and (12).) 
Despite the fact that Council Member's Gamblin's property is separated from 67 Mark 
Drive by a creek, we do not find sufficient facts to indicate that there will be no reasonably 
foreseeable measurable effect on the Council Member's property considering the proximity 
between the properties. While the creek and the fact that the council member cannot view 67 Mark 
Drive from his property evidence that traffic levels and privacy are not neces-;arily significant 
concerns, decisions concerning a potential homeless services center including a homeless shelter 
within such a close proximity could result in a significant change to the character of the parcel and 
measurably impact the value of Council Member Gamblin's property. For example, the decisions 
could substantially alter intensity of use of the property surrounding the Council Member's property 
as well as the noise level, particularly given the likelihood that the site will include a housing 
component (either temporary or longer term) and other outdoor use. (Regulation 18702(a)( I 0).) 
Moreover, participation in a subcommittee to select a site for a homeless service center and 
making a land use decision concerning 67 Mark Drive would cause a reasonably prudent person, 
using due care and consideration under the circumstances, to believe that the governmental 
decision was of such a nature that its reasonably foreseeable effect would influence the market 
value of Council Member Gamblin's real property. (Regulation 18702.2(a)(l 2).) Under the facts 
provided, we find that it is reasonably foreseeable that decisions concerning the location of this 
proposed homeless services center and consideration of a land use application for the center will 
have a material financial effect on the market value of the property. Accordingly, Council Member 
Gamblin is prohibited from participating in decisions regarding the proposed homeless services 
center. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
By: 
Sincerely, 
Hyla P. Wagner 
General Counsel 
IJ.,7:10::: 
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

sections.question:
Does Council Member Gamblin have a conflict of interest in participating in either a council 
subcommittee that reviews potential homeless service center sites, including 67 Mark Drive, or in 
council meetings that involve a decision regarding a land use application for a proposed homeless 
services center at 67 Mark Drive?
sections.conclusion:
Yes. Under the facts provided, participation in a subcommittee and making a land use 
decision concerning 67 Mark Drive will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on 
Council Member Gamblin's real property interest.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does Council Member Gamblin have a conflict of interest in participating in either a council 
subcommittee that reviews potential homeless service center sites, including 67 Mark Drive, or in 
council meetings that involve a decision regarding a land use application for a proposed homeless 
services center at 67 Mark Drive?

CONCLUSION: Yes. Under the facts provided, participation in a subcommittee and making a land use 
decision concerning 67 Mark Drive will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on 
Council Member Gamblin's real property interest.
